data.title: Trump Signals Imminent Fed Chair Pick as Kevin Warsh Emerges Front-Runner
data.description:
The Trump administration is preparing for President Donald Trump to nominate Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve chair, according to people familiar with the matter, with Trump saying Thursday that he plans to announce his pick Friday morning. The decision is not final until a formal announcement is made, the people cautioned, and neither the White House nor Warsh immediately responded to requests for comment. Warsh, a former Fed governor and one of four finalists under consideration, visited the White House on Thursday, while prediction markets showed a notable rise in bets earlier in the day that he could be selected.

Markets appeared to react to those expectations, with stocks moving lower, Treasury yields pushing higher, and the US dollar extending gains as precious metals declined. Trump teased the decision without naming his choice, saying the pick would not be surprising and would be someone known to “everyone” in the financial world, adding that many believe it could be someone who “could've been there a few years ago.” Warsh served on the Federal Reserve's Board of Governors from 2006 to 2011 and has advised Trump on economic policy, and if nominated and confirmed, he would replace Jerome Powell when Powell's term as chair ends in May, marking a potential return after Trump passed him over for the role in 2017.

In recent months, Warsh has aligned more closely with the president by publicly arguing for lower interest rates, a stance that contrasts with his long-standing reputation as an inflation hawk, as Trump has sought a candidate who markets could accept while also favoring faster and deeper rate cuts. The Federal Reserve earlier this week held rates steady after cutting them three times at the end of 2025. Even so, confirmation could prove difficult, with Senator Thom Tillis pledging to block any Fed nominees until the Justice Department concludes its investigation related to the Fed's Washington headquarters renovation and Powell's testimony, and Senate Majority Leader John Thune indicating that confirmation without Tillis would probably not move forward.

data.title:
Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon Plan Massive $60 Billion Investment in OpenAI
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA, Financial), Microsoft (MSFT, Financial), and Amazon (AMZN, Financial) are in discussions to invest as much as $60 billion in OpenAI, as large technology companies look to deepen their exposure to artificial intelligence, according to a Thursday report by The Information.

The report said Nvidia, an existing OpenAI partner whose chips support many AI workloads, is in talks to invest up to $30 billion. Microsoft, a long time backer of OpenAI, is discussing a smaller investment, reported to be below $10 billion.

Amazon, which would be a new investor, is in talks to commit more than $10 billion and potentially over $20 billion. The report said OpenAI is close to receiving term sheets, which outline preliminary investment commitments. None of the companies involved has confirmed a deal.

Amazon's potential investment may hinge on separate negotiations. These include a possible expansion of OpenAI's cloud infrastructure usage on Amazon Web Services and a commercial agreement tied to enterprise ChatGPT offerings.

The talks follow reports earlier this week that SoftBank Group (SFTBY) is exploring an additional investment of up to $30 billion in OpenAI. OpenAI continues to face rising costs to train and operate AI models amid growing competition from Alphabet (GOOGL).

data.title: Amazon Prematurely Alerts Staff to Layoffs in Errant Email
data.description:
Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN, Financials) mistakenly informed some Amazon Web Services employees of impending layoffs hours before official notices were scheduled to go out. The premature email, which referenced Project Dawn, was later retracted after employees reported confusion on internal messaging channels.

The message, signed by Colleen Aubrey, senior vice president of applied AI solutions at AWS, stated that affected staff in the U.S., Canada, and Costa Rica had already been notified of job losses. The accompanying team meeting invitation was quickly canceled.

Amazon plans to lay off up to 30,000 corporate employees, 10% of its office workforce, as it invests in artificial intelligence and decreases management. The company eliminated 14,000 jobs in October, with more cuts planned in AWS, retail, Prime Video, and HR.

As Whole Foods shops combine, Amazon disclosed separate employment cutbacks in its Fresh and Go retail businesses this week.

The corporation has not disclosed the number of last round affected employees. In a previous blog post, Amazon human resources head Beth Galetti tied the restructuring to AI-driven efficiencies, suggesting further layoffs as Amazon automates processes.

data.title: BYD Faces a New Kind of Challenge Abroad
data.description:
BYD Co. (BYDDY) may have a good problem on its hands in India: demand for its electric vehicles is starting to outrun its ability to deliver them.

According to Bloomberg, the Chinese EV maker is actively weighing ways to expand in India as hundreds of customer orders stack up at dealerships. With import quotas and regulatory limits making it harder to ship fully built cars into the country, BYD is now looking at lighter options, including some form of local assembly. People familiar with the talks said the company is also working through safety and certification approvals so it can introduce more models.

India had earlier rejected BYD's proposal to build a full-scale factory, but the company appears to be adjusting its approach. One option under consideration is assembling semi-knocked-down vehicle kits locally, a route that is typically cheaper and easier to clear with regulators. Any move on manufacturing would likely come after a recent visit by senior BYD executives, signaling renewed engagement with Indian officials.

data.title:
Some of Pharma's Biggest Brands Face a New Reality
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Big pharma just got another nudge from Washington that Medicare pricing pressure is only moving in one direction. A new batch of well-known drugs from Pfizer (PFE, Financial), Novartis (NVS, Financial), and Eli Lilly (LLY, Financial) has been pulled into the next round of Medicare price negotiations.

The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services added 15 drugs to its latest negotiation list, including Pfizer's Xeljanz, Novartis' Cosentyx, and Lilly's Trulicity. Other familiar names made the cut too, like AbbVie's Botox, Bristol Myers Squibb's Orencia, and Gilead's Biktarvy. These aren't niche therapies. Between November 2024 and October 2025, about 1.8 million Medicare beneficiaries used the drugs on the list, driving roughly $27 billion in prescription spending.

The key thing to remember is timing. Any negotiated prices won't take effect until 2028, so there's no immediate hit to revenue. But the signal is hard to miss. Medicare is zeroing in on large, established drugs that generate steady cash, and that puts long-term pricing pressure on some of pharma's most dependable products.

data.title: Nvidia and Apple Stocks In Spotlight -- Pelosi Files New Disclosure
data.description:
U.S. Representative Nancy Pelosi disclosed a series of stock and options trades tied to major technology and consumer names, including Nvidia (NVDA, Financial), Apple (AAPL, Financial), Alphabet (GOOG), and Amazon (AMZN, Financial).

The filing shows Pelosi exercised 50 call options on Alphabet with a $150 strike price that she bought in January 2025. She also purchased additional Alphabet call options expiring in January 2027 and transferred thousands of shares to a donor-advised fund.

Pelosi also added exposure to Amazon through call options with a $120 strike price while trimming her equity position. The filing shows sales of Amazon shares in late December, alongside the exercise of previously held options.

Her Apple holdings also shifted. Pelosi sold a large block of shares, added call options expiring in 2027, and contributed shares to a donor-advised fund.

The disclosure showed similar activity in Nvidia, though the filing did not detail the size of the equity adjustment.

data.title: Amazon Wins $581M U.S. Air Force Cloud Deal
data.description:
Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN, Financials) has been awarded a $581.3 million contract from the U.S. Air Force to provide cloud services under its Cloud One Program, the Department of Defense's initiative to modernize and consolidate computing infrastructure.

The firm fixed price contract covers Amazon Web Services cloud support for the Air Force and its affiliated users at AWS sites in the contiguous US. The program improves Air Force cloud computing security, scalability, and efficiency.

The Massachusetts based Air Force Life Cycle Management Center will manage the contract until Dec. 7, 2028. When awarded, $3.5 million from fiscal 2026 operations and maintenance monies was obligated.

AWS competes with Microsoft and Google for government modernization projects in defense and public sector contracts. The Department of Defense's digital transformation activities have increasingly used hybrid and multi cloud infrastructure, including Cloud One.

Investors will scrutinize how the multi year pact boosts AWS's revenue in Amazon's cloud business, a major profit driver. Early February's quarterly earnings report will be the company's next catalyst.

data.title: BYD Co. Targets 1.3 Million Overseas EV Sales in 2026
data.description:
BYD Co. (BYDDF, Financial) is increasingly leaning on international markets as growth conditions at home become more complicated, even after its overseas expansion helped it surpass Tesla (TSLA, Financial) as the world's largest electric-vehicle maker last year. At a media briefing in Shanghai, Li Yunfei, general manager of BYD's brand and public relations division, outlined a target of selling 1.3 million vehicles outside China in 2026, suggesting close to 25% growth from the 1.05 million units delivered overseas last year.

The renewed focus abroad comes as momentum in China appears to be facing headwinds. Demand is coming under pressure as some government incentives supporting EV purchases are being scaled back, while competition among domestic manufacturers continues to intensify. Against that backdrop, overseas markets could be playing a more central role in supporting BYD's growth trajectory, even as the company navigates a tougher and more competitive environment in its home market.

That said, the stated overseas target may land below some prior market expectations. Citigroup said in November that BYD had discussed a goal of expanding overseas sales to between 1.5 million and 1.6 million vehicles in 2026, following meetings with management. The latest guidance could therefore be interpreted as more measured, possibly influencing how investors frame the pace and ambition of BYD's global expansion plans.

data.title: Tesla Q4 Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Upcoming Report
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA, Financial) is all set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report, due Wednesday after the market close, as investors looked for clarity on margins, demand and progress in artificial intelligence initiatives.

Wall Street expects Tesla to report earnings of about $0.45 per share, down from a year earlier, on revenue of roughly $24.8 billion, according to analyst estimates.

The company has faced pressure from repeated vehicle price cuts over the past year, which weighed on profitability. In the third quarter, automotive sales accounted for roughly three-quarters of total revenue, keeping vehicle margins a key focus. Investors are expected to scrutinize automotive gross margin excluding regulatory credits for signs that pricing has stabilized and cost efficiencies are taking hold.

Market attention is also likely to center on updates around Full Self-Driving software, AI compute capacity and longer-term projects such as robotaxis and the Optimus humanoid robot. Management commentary on timelines and adoption could influence near-term sentiment.

Demand trends remain another area of concern. Tesla has seen intensifying competition in China, while sales in Europe fell sharply year over year in 2025, raising questions about global EV momentum.

Options traders are pricing in a sizable move of about 6.3% in either direction for Tesla (TSLA, Financial) around its upcoming Q4 2025 earnings, reflecting elevated expected volatility and uncertainty heading into the report.
